message:
chore: close epics ft-3681t.8 (migration program) and ft-3681t.9 (operator UX)

All children of both epics are now closed:
- ft-3681t.8: parity corpus, shadow comparator, importers, cutover playbook,
  decommission docs, rehearsal suite
- ft-3681t.9: UX validation, command center, session explorer, context budget,
  intervention console, operator runbooks, explainability console

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2835,14 +2835,14 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.4","title":"[ops] Chaos + scale validation harness (1k-pane/connector stress)","description":"Build a rigorous resilience harness covering large pane counts, bursty connector traffic, policy churn, and partial outages to validate SLOs before migration cutover.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Scale tests model realistic swarm + connector load. 2. Chaos scenarios cover key failure classes and recovery. 3. Pass/fail gates are explicit for release decisions.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:36.654720Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:31:03.705579Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","ops","performance","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:28.470529Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:36.654720Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:27.578519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:28.013063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"reason":"Implemented chaos_scale_harness.rs with ScaleProfile (small/medium/large up to 1k panes), 9 FailureClass variants, SLO validation, 23 tests passing. Commit 4a2bb81c."}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.5","title":"[quality] Observability correctness + SLO conformance audit suite","description":"Validate telemetry quality, alert fidelity, dashboard correctness, capacity-governor decisions, and SLO conformance under stress and chaos workloads.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Telemetry and alert streams are accurate, complete, and correlated. 2. Capacity controls demonstrably prevent cascade collapse. 3. SLO audits provide objective release criteria. 4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e/soak tests with detailed structured logs/artifacts.

 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.6","title":"[ops] Disaster recovery backup/restore and control-plane continuity drills","description":"Build and validate backup/restore and continuity procedures for mux/orchestration/policy/connector state so catastrophic failures have a proven recovery path.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkForge","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:45.910771Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:30:00.000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["migration","shadow-mode","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.8.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:45.910771Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.8.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:30.394323Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1943,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.8.2","author":"BlueHorse","text":"Claiming ft-3681t.8.2. Planned slice: wire the existing NTM parity corpus + acceptance matrix into a runnable shadow comparator that executes canonical ft commands, evaluates assertion contracts, and emits the artifact bundle expected by the cutover playbook (summary.json, raw_command_outputs.jsonl, assertion_results.json, divergence report).","created_at":"2026-03-07T07:57:07Z"},{"id":1951,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.8.2","author":"BlueHorse","text":"Progress update: implemented a first `ft replay parity` runner in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` that loads the canonical NTM parity corpus + acceptance matrix, auto-resolves a live pane for `<pane_id>` substitutions, executes the canonical `ft` commands via the current binary, evaluates assertion contracts via `frankenterm_core::ntm_parity`, and writes `summary.json`, `raw_command_outputs.jsonl`, `assertion_results.json`, `shadow_divergence_report.json`, plus per-scenario artifacts under `scenarios/`.

Added a fixture-driven integration test in `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/ntm_parity_runner.rs` and a CLI parse test for `ft replay parity`.

Cargo validation is currently blocked by infrastructure: `rch workers probe --all` shows every worker unreachable, and `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` fails open to local compilation, which I aborted to stay within repo policy.
","created_at":"2026-03-07T08:16:12Z"}],"close_reason":"Implemented in 0e10ae66: DualRunShadowComparator + DriftTriageWorkflow + cutover gates, 29 tests"}

Building ntm_importer.rs: session/workflow/config importers with parity validation and remediation hints.","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:57:57Z"},{"id":2092,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.8.3","author":"PinkForge","text":"Implementation complete. ntm_importer.rs: 2149 lines, 39 unit tests, 17 proptests. Full import pipeline for NTM sessions→profiles+layouts, workflows→translated workflows, config→config fragments. Validation with severity-graded findings and remediation hints. Commits: 6d97bc65 (initial, linter-bundled), d74a5128 (clippy sweep), 6bcc471f (proptests).","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:36:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.8.4","title":"[migration] Staged cutover playbook with rollback gates","description":"Define and automate a staged rollout strategy by team/fleet/environment with progressive canary exposure, blast-radius controls, and rollback checkpoints tied to reliability/security/perf gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Cutover phases and promotion criteria are explicit per environment/team cohort.
2. Progressive exposure (canary cohorts + blast-radius limits) is enforced with automatic halt on guardrail breaches.
3. Rollback triggers are objective, continuously evaluated, and executable within bounded recovery time.
4. Playbook is validated in rehearsals including partial-failure modes and human override drills with signed run logs.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.

Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.

-{"id":"ft-3681t.9","title":"[epic] Human operator UX parity and control-cockpit enhancement","description":"# Epic 9: Human Operator UX Parity and Beyond

Human operator workflows currently handled in NTM have first-class FrankenTerm-native UX paths.

4. Comprehensive unit/integration/e2e coverage with detailed structured logs and artifact capture is enforced for all shipped UX flows.

Q5.  {"id":"ft-3681t.9.3","title":"[ux] Session/workflow explorer with timeline replay and extraction tools","description":"Provide deep session/workflow exploration: timeline replay, diffing checkpoints, extracting key events/errors/actions, and linking directly into robot/workflow interventions.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Explorer supports fast root-cause and state reconstruction workflows. 2. Replay/extract operations are deterministic and reproducible. 3. Linked interventions reduce context-switch overhead. 4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e replay tests and detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5.
